title: Kaboom Crew polish round 2 + engine perf gates + asset inventory probe

stories.title: Audio volume dial — query param + localStorage persistence
stories.summary: Project-friendly volume control. `?audio=off` mutes; `?audio=0..1` sets master gain; default `1.0`. Persist the last-applied value in localStorage under `agf.audio.volume` so a reload keeps the setting. KaboomAudioFx already accepts masterGain; this story wires the URL + storage read on attachUi and re-applies on the same reset hook the difficulty preset uses.
verification: unit test: parser handles 'off' / '0.4' / '' / 'bad' / out-of-range; localStorage round-trip
live probe: ?audio=off → audio-fx returns silent stubs; reload without the param → muted state persists from localStorage
parseAudioVolumeParam + resolveAudioVolume helpers in audio-fx.ts; +12 unit tests covering parsing edge cases / URL→storage persistence / corruption fallback
bootstrap resolves the dial on attachUi (URL → localStorage → default 1) and scales the existing 0.4 SFX baseline by it; ?audio=off mutes immediately and persists across reloads

stories.title: Auto-emit AGF_FRAME_SPIKE warning when frame > 50 ms
stories.summary: Engine-side passive perf gate. The runtime already samples per-phase frame timing into a window (M21-frame-timing). Add a guard that emits `severity: 'warning', code: 'AGF_FRAME_SPIKE'` once per spike — defined as totalFrameMs > spikeMs (default 50). Cooldown 1 s between emissions so a stuck frame doesn't spam the bus. Configurable via `RuntimeOptions.spikeMs`. Stays off when set to 0.
verification: unit test: synthetic frame-timing sequence with a spike triggers one emission; sustained spikes throttle to ≤ 1/sec
boot-noise-budget smoke test still green on hello-3d (steady-state has no spikes)
RuntimeOptions { spikeMs?: number = 50; spikeCooldownMs?: number = 1000 } threaded into the main tick; spikeMs=0 disables
AGF_FRAME_SPIKE warning emitted with { totalMs, threshold } in details; cooldown rate-limits a stuck frame to ≤ 1/sec

stories.title: Particle puff when a bomber dies
stories.summary: Mirror the S84 blast-burst pattern on death. When `BomberStats.alive` flips from true to false, the project-local audio-binding system already emits 'death'; add a sibling effect: spawn a short-lived 'glow' preset emitter (capacity 10, lifetime 0.5 s) at the bomber's grid cell. Gives the round-end moment some visual weight.
verification: unit test: simulated death emits a ParticleEmitter entity at the bomber's cell within one fixedUpdate tick
live probe: BomberStats.alive=false applied via /__agf/commands produces a visible puff
audio-binding-system spawns a  ParticleEmitter entity (preset='glow', lifetime=0.5s, rate=30, maxParticles=10) at the dead bomber's grid cell, in the same fixedUpdate where it emits 'death'
ParticleEmitterSystem owns lifetime cleanup; no manual delete needed

stories.title: Second arena preset — `?map=wide` (17x13 cells)
stories.summary: Second hand-tuned arena variant. `examples/kaboom-crew/scenes/wide.scene.json` (17 × 13 cells, more soft blocks, bots start further apart). Selectable via `?map=wide` (default `start`). Schema-level changes are zero — the grid bounds live in the Grid singleton component already.
verification: wide.scene.json + soft-block instances laid out; engine check passes
live probe: ?map=wide boots a 17x13 arena; bot.1 spawns at (14,11)

stories.title: /__agf/asset-inventory — list every loaded / pending asset ref
stories.summary: New GET endpoint on the dev bridge. Returns every asset the runtime touched: `{ ref, kind, status }[]` where status ∈ 'loaded' | 'pending' | 'failed'. Pairs with the existing /__agf/asset/invalidate POST — agents working an HMR issue get a complete inventory before deciding what to bust. Mirrors the rendererInspect / diagnostics pattern.
verification: endpoint registered on dev-bridge; unit test on the AssetRegistry inventory helper
live probe: curl /__agf/asset-inventory on kaboom-crew returns the 5 prefab JSONs + any loaded glb / material refs

stories.title: Lift material-binding-system console sites to runtime.diagnostics
stories.summary: S85 closed renderer-adapter sites but the material-binding-system file-level allow marker stayed because the system didn't have a bus reference. Thread `{ diagnostics? }` into the system's deps options + lift the 5 warn/error sites (mesh-load failure, mesh has no Mesh, material-load failure, material has no kind, applied to unknown handle). Drop the file-level marker.
verification: options { diagnostics? } threaded; 5 sites emit AGF_RENDER_MATERIAL_* / AGF_RENDER_GEOMETRY_* events
check-no-console-log gate passes without the material-binding-system file marker

stories.title: Esc opens a pause menu — Resume / Restart / Difficulty cycle
stories.summary: Centre HUD widget with three clickable rows. Esc toggles. Resume removes GamePaused (same pattern as the title screen). Restart calls restartScene. Difficulty cycles easy → normal → hard, applies the preset immediately to bot.1 + writes it into `?difficulty=` via history.replaceState so a reload preserves it.
verification: live probe: Esc mounts the menu, GamePaused added to kaboom.game-state; clicking Resume removes GamePaused; clicking Restart triggers scene.load
Difficulty button cycles through 3 presets — current label visible on the button

stories.title: engine doctor surfaces sprint-level follow-ups
stories.summary: Tail of every archived sprint JSON's `followUps[]` (free-text strings) is interesting next-up reading; right now an agent has to grep them by hand. Add a `followUps: { sprintId, text }[]` block to DoctorReport (collected from backlog/sprints/*.sprint.json) so `engine doctor --json` surfaces the running list. Cap at 20 most recent.
verification: DoctorReport.followUps populated from the JSON files; unit test on a fixture set
format pass surfaces a 'Follow-ups:' section so plain text doctor output isn't silent
